rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. GABRIEL RUEDIGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION Denied. The levying officer is directed to release the levied funds of $ 279.42 from Wells Fargo Bank to the judgment creditor, Discover Bank, fka Greenwood Trust Company. Claimant has not demonstrated that the levied funds are exempt pursuant to CCP §§ 704.010, 704.020, 704.040, or 704.740,. (Claim of Exemption, 4.) Although Claimant has provided a Financial Statement, Claimant does not make his claim on the basis hat the property is necessary for his support. (Claim of Exemption, 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC. A VS. ALBERT L BOASBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC. A, RAY SHAHANI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled in part and sustained in part. As to the first and second causes of action, Cross-Defendants' demurrer is overruled. As to the third cause of action, Cross-Defendants demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant has not, and cannot show, that there was a previous litigation that was terminated in her favor. (Babb v. Superior Court (1971) 3 Cal.3d 841, 845-846.) The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is moot because Cross-Complainant dismissed the claim on January 6, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Defs' Responses To Pltf'S Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S Motion To Compel Defendants' Responses To Plaintiff's Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND SCOTT MUNRO TO PROVIDE RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS-SET TWO AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES-SET TWO AS TO UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES-SET ONE TO SCOTT MUNRO IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A SEPARATE STATEMENT ATTACHING THE DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO EITHER THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OR THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO WHICH IT IS SEEKING ANSWERS. WITHOUT SUCH A STATEMENT THE COURT HAS NO BASIS ON WHICH TO MAKE A RULING. IN ADDITION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF MADE NO GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT TO MEET AND CONFER FOLLOWING RECEIPT OF DEFENDANTS' COUNSELS' LETTER OF JANUARY. SEE CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT, RULE 3.1345. BECAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S PRO PER STATUS THE COURT WILL NOT IMPOSE ANY MONETARY SANCTIONS; HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF IS CAUTIONED THAT ANY FURTHER MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY WHICH FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA STATUTES WILL BE SUBJECT TO MONETARY SANCTIONS. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO AND SAKSEE ISANPAYU VS. 2001 THAI STICK, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Omnibus Mtn For Discovery Relief And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO and SAKSEE ISANPAYU'S Omnibus Motion For Discovery Relief And Request For Monetary Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: 1. DEFENDANTS 2001 THAI STICK, INC ("THAI STICK") AND POLEMAHASUPPAPOLE ( "PAUL POLE") (COLLECTIVELY "DEFENDANTS") OBJECTIONS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7 & 2.8 ARE OVERRULED AND THEY WILL SERVE FULL AND COMPLETE ANSWERS, WITHOUT OBJECTION TO SAID INTERROGATORIES. 2. AS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 2.5, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 3.7, 4.1, 4.2, 7.5, 9.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1 & 15.1 DEFENDANTS DID RESPOND. PLAINTIFFS HAVE MADE NO SHOWING THAT THE RESPONSES ARE INCOMPLETE OTHER THAN TO ARGUE THAT THE OBJECTIONS WERE UNTIMELY AND WITHOUT MERIT. THE COURT WILL MAKE NO FURTHER ORDER REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO FURTHER RESPOND. 3. THAI STICK IS ORDERED TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO ALL OF PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WHICH RESPONSES WILL BE IN THE FORM SPECIFIED IN CCP - ADMIT, DENY OR EXPRESS AN INABILITY TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY AFTER REASONABLE INQUIRY. 4. TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY OF THAI STICK'S RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ARE OTHER THAN AN OUTRIGHT ADMISSION, THAI STICK WILL SERVE WITHOUT OBJECTIONS FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 AND IN SO DOING SHALL CORRECTLY IDENTIFY THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSION TO WHICH ITS RESPONSES APPLY. 5. THAI STICK IS ORDERED TO SERVE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 1, 2 & 20 AS TO ANY EMPLOYEE EMPLOYED BY ANY RESTAURANT OWNED BY DEFENDANT THAI STICK DURING THE PERIOD OF PLAINTIFFS EMPLOYMENT. 6. TO THE EXTENT IT HAS NOT ALREADY DONE SO, THAI STICK WILL PRODUCE ALL OF THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED IN PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITH THE EXCEPTION OF REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 5, 6, 25, 35, 41, 42, 47, 48, 49 & 52 WHICH CAN AWAIT A DETERMINATION AS TO THE APPROPRIATE CLASS, IF ANY, IN THIS LITIGATION. 7. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,000 TO BE PAID TO PLAINTIFFS. 8. DEFENDANTS SHALL SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES, PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND PAY SANCTIONS WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. (See Part 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2005-3 VS. ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled. (CCP § 430.10(e).) Ms. Polakovic has pled sufficient facts to allege violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) and the Rosenthal Debt Collection Practices Act. The allegations are not clearly barred by the statute of limitations on the face of the Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's other arguments involve questions of fact, that are not subject to demurrer. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. Cross-Defendant did not cite to any California legal authority in support of its request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2006-4, VS. ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled. (CCP § 430.10(e).) Ms. Polakovic has pled sufficient facts to allege violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) and the Rosenthal Debt Collection Practices Act. The allegations are not clearly barred by the statute of limitations on the face of the Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's other arguments involve questions of fact, that are not subject to demurrer. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. Cross-Defendant did not cite to any California legal authority in support of its request. rulings.Case Title: JOSE ANAYA VS. DULCE MARIA ESPINOZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT DULCE ESPINOZA'S Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THERE APPEARS TO BE NO OPPOSITION PAPERS FILED IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT CULCE MARIA EXPINOZA'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL GRANT DEFENDANT'S MOTION AND IMPOSE SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 WHICH WILL BE PAID BY PLAINTIFF WITHIN TO (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE ORDER SUBMITTED AS AMENDED BY THE TERMS OF THIS INTENDED DECISION. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PADWAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MICHAEL VS. NAPOLI, BERN,RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Napoli Bern Ripka & Associates, Llp'S Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And Dba Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT NAPOLI BERN RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLP'S Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And DBA Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, was assigned to hear the underlying motion. On January 6, 2014, the parties stipulated to Pro Tem Judge Renne hearing the underlying motion that is the subject of reconsideration. The parties are deemed to have stipulated to the authority of Pro Tem Judge Renne for the instant motion for reconsideration. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT' MOTION TO BE RELIEVED FROM THIS COURT'S PRIOR ORDER RE: WAIVER OFF PRIVILEGE OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IT GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT HAS MADE A SHOWING OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CALIF. CODE OF CIVIL PROC. SECTION 473(b). NOTHING IN THIS ORDER IS INTENDED TO ADDRESS THE MERITS OF DEFENDANT'S PRIVILEGE CLAIMS. THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 IF AFFIRMED AND PAYMENT SHOULD BE MADE TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN TEN (DAYS) FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT TO THE COURT OF SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON FEBRUARY 25, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LONG VS. FORTY NINERS FOOTBALL COMPANY, LLC, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Forty Niners Football Company, Llc'S Amended Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014, Line 21, Defendant Forty Niners Football Company, LLC's Amended Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Defendant's general demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's first cause of action for breach of contract fails to state sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action. The stadium ticket and parking pass are fully integrated and contain unambiguous merger clauses prohibiting the admission of extrinsic evidence to clarify their terms. Plaintiff's second, third, and fourth causes of action for negligence, malice and punitive damages, and rescue doctrine are time-barred under C.C.P. § 335.1 and the action was not tolled during the pendency of Plaintiff's federal claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
